comment_text.sn21.4:1.2: The rare verb saṅkasāyati is used alongside allīyissanti (Kd 3:1.2.3) suggesting the sense “adhere” and a derivation from kasāya (“gum, resin”). | The time for sewing robes was the kathina season after the rains residence (MN 122:2.15).
comment_text.sn21.4:6.4: The ten armies of Māra are detailed in Snp 3.2.

root_text.sn21.4:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro navo bhikkhu pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto vihāraṁ pavisitvā appossukko tuṇhībhūto saṅkasāyati, na bhikkhūnaṁ veyyāvaccaṁ karoti cīvarakārasamaye.
root_text.sn21.4:1.3: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn21.4:1.4: “idha, bhante, aññataro navo bhikkhu pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto vihāraṁ pavisitvā appossukko tuṇhībhūto saṅkasāyati, na bhikkhūnaṁ veyyāvaccaṁ karoti cīvarakārasamaye”ti.
root_text.sn21.4:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn21.4:2.2: “ehi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mama vacanena taṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantehi ‘satthā taṁ, āvuso, āmantetī’”ti.
root_text.sn21.4:2.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato paṭissutvā yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn21.4:2.5: “Evamāvuso”ti kho so bhikkhu tassa bhikkhuno paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ bhikkhuṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn21.4:2.6: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto vihāraṁ pavisitvā appossukko tuṇhībhūto saṅkasāyasi, na bhikkhūnaṁ veyyāvaccaṁ karosi cīvarakārasamaye”ti?
root_text.sn21.4:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā tassa bhikkhuno cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn21.4:3.2: “mā kho tumhe, bhikkhave, etassa bhikkhuno ujjhāyittha.
root_text.sn21.4:3.3: Eso kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, yassa catthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti, tadanuttaraṁ brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī”ti.
root_text.sn21.4:4.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn21.4:1.2: Now at that time a certain junior monk, after his meal, on his return from almsround, entered his dwelling, where he adhered to passivity and silence. And he didn’t help the mendicants out when it was time to sew robes.
translation_text.sn21.4:1.3: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.sn21.4:2.1: So the Buddha addressed one of the monks,
translation_text.sn21.4:2.2: “Please, monk, in my name tell that monk that the Teacher summons him.”
translation_text.sn21.4:2.3: “Yes, sir,” that monk replied. He went to that monk and said to him,
translation_text.sn21.4:2.5: “Yes, reverend,” that monk replied. He went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn21.4:2.6: “Is it really true, monk, that after your meal, on your return from almsround, you entered your dwelling, where you adhered to passivity and silence, and you didn’t help the mendicants out when it was time to sew robes?”
translation_text.sn21.4:2.7: “Sir, I am doing my own work.”
translation_text.sn21.4:3.1: Then the Buddha, knowing that monk’s train of thought, addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn21.4:3.3: This monk gets the four absorptions—blissful meditations in this life that belong to the higher mind—when he wants, without trouble or difficulty. He has realized the supreme culmination of the spiritual path in this very life, and lives having achieved with his own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.”
translation_text.sn21.4:4.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn21.4:4.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn21.4:6.4: having vanquished Māra with his legions.”
